input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule T.2.4 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.2.4
            TITLE: belt, strap and harness installation - general

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.4: Belt, Strap and Harness Installation - General
T.2.4 Belt, Strap and Harness Installation - General

RULE: t.2.4.2
            TITLE: any guide or support for the belts must be material meeting f.3.2.1.j

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.4.2: Any guide or support for the belts must be material meeting F.3.2.1.j
T.2.4.2 Any guide or support for the belts must be material meeting F.3.2.1.j

RULE: t.2.4.1
            TITLE: the lap belt, shoulder belts and anti-submarine belt(s) must be securely mounted to the

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.4.1: The Lap Belt, Shoulder Belts and Anti-Submarine Belt(s) must be securely mounted to the
T.2.4.1 The Lap Belt, Shoulder Belts and Anti-Submarine Belt(s) must be securely mounted to the 
Primary Structure.

RULE: t.2.4.3
            TITLE: each tab or bracket to which any part of the harness is attached must:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.4.3: Each tab or bracket to which any part of the Harness is attached must:
T.2.4.3 Each tab or bracket to which any part of the Harness is attached must: 
a. Support a minimum load in pullout and tearout before failure of:  
• If one belt is attached to the tab or bracket    15 kN 
• If two belts are attached to the tab or bracket   30 kN 
b. Be 1.6 mm minimum thickness 
c. Not be in bending when the attached part of the Harness is put under load  
d. Not cause abrasion to the belt webbing

RULE: t.2.4.4
            TITLE: attachment of tabs or brackets must meet the following:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.4.4: Attachment of tabs or brackets must meet the following:
T.2.4.4 Attachment of tabs or brackets must meet the following: 
a. Where brackets are fastened to the chassis, no less than two 6 mm or 1/4” minimum 
diameter Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2 or stronger must be used to attach the bracket to 
the chassis. 
b. Where a single shear tab is welded to the chassis, the tab to tube welding must be on 
both sides of the base of the tab. 
Double shear attachments are preferred.  Tabs and brackets for double shear mounts should 
be welded on both sides.

final_answer: T.2.4 Belt, Strap and Harness Installation - General
